place: Vernon Center CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Vernon Center once thrived as a mercantile hub, with grist mills, sawmills, a tannery, cheese factories powered by Town Brook, and stores like the Denton family shop and Vandegriff's Tavern.
The village began as a slope‑side vacation housing development of 1,356 units, now operated by real‑estate investor Andrew Mulvihill.
Vernon Township was created on April 8 1793 from Hardyston Township and named after George Washington’s half‑brother Lawrence Washington’s estate, Mount Vernon.
In 2003, Vernon Center earned a Town Center Designation to promote smart growth while preserving over 4,000 years of architectural history in buildings like the Abram Rutan House.
The 1881 Lehigh & Hudson River Railroad Depot, now a deli, showcases the township’s historic rail connection.
Vernon Township hosts Mountain Creek, a ski resort that once operated as Great Gorge and now also offers a water park.
